projects.title: Unit 9 Project — Coordinate Plane & Integers in Action
projects.desc: Apply graphing, integers, and the coordinate plane to a real-world capstone challenge for Unit 9.

projects.title: Archipelago Coordinate Navigator
projects.desc: Plot ordered pairs across all four quadrants in a pirate quest (6.NS.6b).

projects.title: Coordinate Treasure Hunt
projects.desc: Find treasure by plotting ordered pairs with signed coordinates (6.NS.6).

projects.title: Treasure Map Navigator (Part 2)
projects.desc: Interactive graphic novel: ordered pairs in all four quadrants (6.NS.6b).

title: Ordered Pairs in All Four Quadrants
contentObjective: I can plot ordered pairs in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane.
languageObjective: I can explain my work using the words quadrant, negative coordinate, reflection, and axis.

turnAndTalk.question: What do the signs of the coordinates in an ordered pair tell you about which quadrant the point is in?
turnAndTalk.kernel: The signs of the coordinates tell me the quadrant because ___.

stems.en: If both coordinates are positive, the point is in quadrant ___.
stems.es: Si ambas coordenadas son positivas, el punto esta en el cuadrante ___.

stems.en: If the x is negative and the y is positive, the point is in quadrant ___.
stems.es: Si la x es negativa y la y es positiva, el punto esta en el cuadrante ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student matches sign combinations to quadrants (e.g., (+,+) is I, (-,+) is II).
turnAndTalk.extend: Push students to explain how to tell which quadrant a point is in without plotting it.
extendStems: I can find the quadrant without plotting by ___.
The signs of (x, y) tell me ___.

turnAndTalk.question: Plot (-3, -4). Which quadrant is it in, and how do the negative coordinates change the directions you move from the origin?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Negative coordinates change my moves because ___.

stems.en: A negative x means I move ___.
stems.es: Una x negativa significa que me muevo ___.

stems.en: A negative y means I move ___.
stems.es: Una y negativa significa que me muevo ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student moves left for negative x and down for negative y, landing in quadrant III.
turnAndTalk.extend: Ask students to predict the quadrant of (-3, 4) and compare how it differs from (-3, -4).
extendStems: (-3, 4) is in quadrant ___, but (-3, -4) is in quadrant ___ because ___.
Changing the sign of y moves the point from ___ to ___.

turnAndTalk.question: How are the four quadrants like a tic-tac-toe of positive and negative directions on the coordinate plane?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Each quadrant is a combination of ___ and ___.

stems.es: El cuadrante ___ tiene ambas coordenadas positivas.
El cuadrante ___ tiene ambas coordenadas negativas.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student connects each quadrant to a unique sign pattern for x and y.
turnAndTalk.extend: Push students to generalize what happens to a point that lands exactly on an axis instead of inside a quadrant.
extendStems: A point on the x-axis has a y-coordinate of ___.
A point on an axis is not in any quadrant because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: A point has coordinates (5, -2). Without plotting, how can you describe exactly where it is and which quadrant it is in?
turnAndTalk.kernel: The point (5, -2) is in quadrant ___ because ___.

stems.en: The point is ___ of the y-axis and ___ of the x-axis.
stems.es: El punto esta a la ___ del eje y y ___ del eje x.

stems.en: It is in quadrant ___ because the signs are ___.
stems.es: Esta en el cuadrante ___ porque los signos son ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student says (5, -2) is right and down, in quadrant IV, from the signs alone.
turnAndTalk.extend: Ask students to critique a classmate who said (5, -2) is in quadrant I and explain the error.
extendStems: The negative y-coordinate means the point must be ___.

turnAndTalk.question: During practice on Ordered Pairs in All Four Quadrants, what strategy did you use when a problem felt tricky?
turnAndTalk.kernel: The signs of (x, y) tell you the quadrant: (+,+) is I, (-,+) is II, (-,-) is III, (+,-) is IV.

stems.en: A negative x means I move ___.
stems.es: Una x negativa significa que me muevo ___.

stems.en: I knew my answer was reasonable because ___.
stems.es: Supe que mi respuesta era razonable porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Listen for students naming a specific strategy tied to 6.NS.6b — not just "I multiplied." They should connect steps to the key idea.
turnAndTalk.extend: How would you help a classmate who made the common mistake from today's lesson? Use one vocabulary word.
extendStems: I would tell them to ___ first because ___.
The mistake happens when you ___ instead of ___.

vocabulary.definition: One of the four parts of a coordinate grid, numbered I to IV.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una de las cuatro partes de una cuadrícula de coordenadas, numeradas I a IV.
vocabulary.visual: I (+, +) top-right, II (-, +) top-left, III (-, -) bottom-left, IV (+, -) bottom-right
vocabulary.cloze: One of the four regions of the coordinate plane, numbered I to IV, is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A coordinate less than zero. It means left or below the center point.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una coordenada menor que cero. Significa a la izquierda o debajo del centro.
vocabulary.visual: (-3, 2) means 3 left and 2 up from the origin
vocabulary.cloze: A coordinate less than zero, which places a point left of or below the origin, is a ___.

examples.why: It is a coordinate less than zero.

vocabulary.definition: A flipped, mirror image of a point across a line.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una imagen reflejada de un punto al otro lado de una línea.
vocabulary.visual: (3, 2) reflected over the y-axis is (-3, 2) — same distance, opposite side
vocabulary.cloze: A flip of a point over an axis is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A line on the grid. The x-axis goes across; the y-axis goes up.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una línea en la cuadrícula. El eje x va de lado; el eje y va hacia arriba.
vocabulary.visual: The x-axis goes left-right; the y-axis goes up-down; they cross at (0, 0)
vocabulary.cloze: One of the two number lines, the x-axis or y-axis, that form the grid is an ___.

vocabulary.definition: Whole numbers and their opposites, like -2, -1, 0, 1, 2.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Números enteros y sus opuestos, como -2, -1, 0, 1, 2.
vocabulary.cloze: A whole number or its opposite is an ___.

launch.narrative: Captain Vega realized her treasure map only covered one corner of the island! She needs to expand the map in all directions — north, south, east, and west of the base camp at the origin (0, 0). Now clues can have negative coordinates: (-4, 3) means 4 units west and 3 units north. A clue at (2, -5) means 2 units east and 5 units south. Which quadrant is each clue in?
launch.contextImage: Full island treasure map with coordinate grid extending in all four directions from a central base camp
noticePrompts: How do you know which quadrant a point is in by looking at the signs of its coordinates?
What do negative x-values and negative y-values mean on the map?
Where is the point (0, 0) on the expanded map?
wonderPrompts: Can a point be in more than one quadrant?
What quadrant is a point on if one of its coordinates is zero?

conceptIntro.heading: How do you plot points in all four quadrants?
conceptIntro.intro: The coordinate plane has four parts called quadrants. A negative coordinate means you move left (for x) or down (for y) from the origin instead of right or up.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: The signs of (x, y) tell you the quadrant: (+,+) is I, (-,+) is II, (-,-) is III, (+,-) is IV.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: I will plot (-3, -4). I start at the origin (0, 0).
The x is -3, which is negative, so I move 3 units to the LEFT.
The y is -4, which is negative, so I move 4 units DOWN. I place my dot.
Both coordinates are negative, so the point is in Quadrant III.

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: Now let's plot (-5, 2). The x is -5, so which way do we move? Left 5 units.
The y is 2, which is positive, so which way? Up 2 units.
Left and up lands us in which quadrant? Quadrant II.

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: In the explore, plot each treasure clue like (4, 3) and (-5, 2) and name the quadrant it lands in.

explore.instructions: Plot each treasure clue on the expanded map and identify which quadrant it falls in.
explore.label: Plot each point and label its quadrant.

discourse.prompt: How do the signs of the x- and y-coordinates tell you which quadrant a point is in?
discourse.sentenceFrame: In Quadrant ___, the x-coordinate is ___ and the y-coordinate is ___. I know (-3, -4) is in Quadrant ___ because both coordinates are ___.

optional.instructions: Captain Vega spotted four enemy ships. Drag each ship's coordinates into the correct quadrant of the map.

optional.stem: A sonar ping locates an enemy submarine at (-5, -2). In which quadrant should Captain Vega aim her shot?
optional.explanation: A negative x means left and a negative y means down, so (-5, -2) is in Quadrant III, where both coordinates are negative.

optional.stem: Captain Vega's lookout reports a ship in Quadrant II. What signs will its coordinates (x, y) have?
optional.explanation: Quadrant II is up and to the left of the origin, so x is negative and y is positive: (-, +).

optional.label: Match each enemy ship's coordinates to the quadrant where Captain Vega should fire.

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — The Misfired Shot

workedExample.label: Target
workedExample.work: An enemy ship is at (-3, 4). In which quadrant should Captain Vega fire?

workedExample.label: Gunner's thinking
workedExample.work: Ignore the signs and just use the numbers 3 and 4. Both numbers are there, so it must be a bottom-left target.

optional.correctWork: You cannot ignore the signs! The x is -3 (negative = left) and the y is 4 (positive = up). Left and up is Quadrant II, not Quadrant III. Captain Vega should fire on Quadrant II.
hints: Look at the SIGN of each coordinate, not just the number. What does a negative x mean — left or right?
A negative x and a positive y point you left and up. Which quadrant is in the top-left of the map?

approaching.stem: In which quadrant is the point (-4, 5)?
approaching.explanation: The x-coordinate is negative (left) and the y-coordinate is positive (up). Left and up is Quadrant II.

approaching.stem: A point has a positive x-coordinate and a negative y-coordinate. Which quadrant is it in?
approaching.explanation: Positive x (right) and negative y (down) = Quadrant IV (bottom-right).

approaching.stem: Which point is located on the x-axis?
approaching.explanation: A point is on the x-axis when its y-coordinate is 0. (4, 0) is on the x-axis.

approaching.label: Match each ordered pair to its quadrant.
Sort these points by their quadrant: I first, then II, III, IV.

approaching.title: approaching — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Ordered Pairs in All Four Quadrants.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: The signs of (x, y) tell you the quadrant: (+,+) is I, (-,+) is II, (-,-) is III, (+,-) is IV.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

approaching.correctWork: Follow the key idea: The signs of (x, y) tell you the quadrant: (+,+) is I, (-,+) is II, (-,-) is III, (+,-) is IV.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: The signs of (x, y) tell you the quadrant: (+,+) is I, (-,+) is II, (-,-) is III, (+,-) is IV.

onLevel.label: Plot one point in each quadrant: (3, 2), (-4, 1), (-2, -3), (5, -4)

onLevel.instructions: For each ordered pair, write the signs of x and y and name the quadrant (or axis).

onLevel.label: Match each quadrant to its coordinate sign pattern.

onLevel.stem: Point A is at (-5, 3). If you move 8 units to the right, what are the new coordinates?
onLevel.explanation: Moving right means adding to the x-coordinate: -5 + 8 = 3. The y stays the same. New point: (3, 3).

onLevel.title: onLevel — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Ordered Pairs in All Four Quadrants.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: The signs of (x, y) tell you the quadrant: (+,+) is I, (-,+) is II, (-,-) is III, (+,-) is IV.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

onLevel.correctWork: Follow the key idea: The signs of (x, y) tell you the quadrant: (+,+) is I, (-,+) is II, (-,-) is III, (+,-) is IV.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: The signs of (x, y) tell you the quadrant: (+,+) is I, (-,+) is II, (-,-) is III, (+,-) is IV.

extending.title: Find the Quadrant Error

workedExample.label: Student thinking
workedExample.work: The x is -3, so I go left 3. The y is -5, so I go up 5.

extending.correctWork: A negative y-coordinate means DOWN, not up. Go left 3 and DOWN 5. The point (-3, -5) is in Quadrant III (both negative), not Quadrant II.
hints: What direction does a NEGATIVE y-coordinate go — up or down?
Both coordinates are negative. In which quadrant are both x and y negative?

extending.prompt: A point starts in Quadrant I at (4, 6). Describe a move that puts it in Quadrant III. What must happen to both coordinates? Can you give the exact new coordinates after your move?
extending.sentenceFrame: To move from Quadrant I to Quadrant III, both coordinates must become ___. Starting at (4, 6), I could move ___ units left and ___ units down to reach (___,___), which is in Quadrant III because ___.

extending.label: Plot these 4 points and connect them in order to form a shape. Name the shape and identify which quadrants it spans: (-3, 2), (3, 2), (3, -2), (-3, -2)

optionalActivity.name: Four-Quadrant Battleship
optionalActivity.intro: Captain Vega's fleet is hidden across all four quadrants of the treasure map — call out the coordinates to land each shot!

practice.commonMistake: A common mistake in Ordered Pairs in All Four Quadrants is skipping the key idea: "The signs of (x, y) tell you the quadrant: (+,+) is I, (-,+) is II, (-,-) is III, (+,-) is IV." — always check your work against this rule before you submit.

connect.scenario: A city uses a coordinate grid to map locations. City Hall is at the origin (0, 0). The library is at (-3, 4), the park is at (5, -2), the school is at (2, 3), and the hospital is at (-4, -1).
connect.promptQuestion: How do four-quadrant coordinates help a city map locations in every direction from the center?
connect.prompt: The library is in Quadrant ___ because its x is ___ and its y is ___. The park is in Quadrant ___ because ___. Four-quadrant coordinates help because ___.

exitTicket.stem: A point has a negative x-coordinate and a negative y-coordinate. Which quadrant is it in?
exitTicket.explanation: Quadrant III is where both x and y are negative. This means the point is left of the origin and below the origin.

printables.name: Four-Quadrant Battleship
printables.desc: Students name quadrants from ordered pairs while playing four-quadrant battleship.

printables.name: Ordered Pairs in All Four Quadrants — Color by Number
printables.desc: Solve each problem, then color by the answer to reveal a picture.
